title: Thoughts on Rebuilds Tokyo 3
wikitext:
[]: Thoughts on Rebuild's Tokyo-3             Quote Post by quixote77 » Thu Jul 16, 2009 11:41 pm Personally, I'm loving it, especially all the exposition on the city in 2.0. Rather than a glorified cold war-esque bomb-shelter in the original series, Tokyo-3 in Rebuild is seeming and alive with modern yet somehow eye-pleasing architecture and people going about their lives and working, creating the image of a bustling metropolis. In other words, it seems like a city genuinely worth fighting for, and those that dwell within are given better movement and definition. Because one has to acknowledge that the city in the series was a ghost town half of the time, probably keeping consistent with the series' depressing themes of isolation and thanatos. I see this as an indicator of two things; more upbeat world for a more upbeat story or a potential sacrificial lamb for a future apocalyptic disaster. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Fri Jul 17, 2009 12:06 am The people there seem pretty happy even though there are giant human-esque creators running around fighting giant walking clocks.

[]: Re: Thoughts on Rebuild's Tokyo-3             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 17, 2009 1:48 am quixote77 wrote: the city in the series was a ghost town half of the time

Rather than a glorified cold war-esque bomb-shelter in the original series, Tokyo-3 in Rebuild is seeming and alive with modern yet somehow eye-pleasing architecture and people going about their lives and working, creating the image of a bustling metropolis. In other words, it seems like a city genuinely worth fighting for, and those that dwell within are given better movement and definition. Because one has to acknowledge that the city in the series was a ghost town half of the time, probably keeping consistent with the series' depressing themes of isolation and thanatos. I see this as an indicator of two things; more upbeat world for a more upbeat story or a potential sacrificial lamb for a future apocalyptic disaster. Thoughts?
